concepts.title: Galois representations and examples
concepts.blurb: Continuous homomorphisms to linear groups encode arithmetic symmetries.

concepts.title: Irreducibility and semisimplification
concepts.blurb: Frobenius traces only see the semisimplification ρ^{ss}, the direct sum of Jordan–Hölder factors; irreducibility is the generic case.

concepts.title: Frobenius traces and compatibility
concepts.blurb: At unramified primes, Frobenius characteristic polynomials carry local arithmetic data.

concepts.title: Inertia, ramification, and the conductor
concepts.blurb: Ramified primes are those where inertia acts nontrivially; the conductor exponent records the size of the inertia invariants.

concepts.title: Residual reps, deformations, modularity
concepts.blurb: Deformation theory and modularity transfer results connect Galois and automorphic worlds.
